data.body:
DONE — Jira link/relation sync implemented end-to-end and LIVE-VERIFIED.
- Cycle 1 (bbad01da): fixed broken client primitives — REST-based get_issue_links (acli link list does not return link targets), dropped --json from set_relationship/delete_issue_link (ACLI rejects/hangs), relation<->Jira-link-type maps.
- Cycle 2 (f6687adf): wired both differs — outbound emits ADD link ops from deps[] with churn-safe dedup vs snapshot issuelinks; inbound reflects Jira issuelinks into rebar relations with direction handling + echo-suppression. Both in-process TDD specs green (xfail removed).
- Cycle 3 (30d5cfc1): apply paths — outbound calls set_relationship (link-only update no longer a no-op; non-idempotent-retry duplicate risk documented + tied to d843); inbound writes via rebar.link facade (idempotent via redundant-link guard). links ride the existing update action (no new MutationAction).
COMPREHENSIVE LIVE ROUND-TRIP (tests/external/test_link_sync_roundtrip_live.py, REBAR_RUN_EXTERNAL-gated): against real Jira created a Blocks link via the apply leaf, proved differ dedup vs live state (no duplicate), read it back through the inbound differ with correct direction (A inwardIssue=B => 'A blocks B'). 1 passed, 9.3s, zero orphans. Full suite green (broad 1770 + integration 17 + 9 apply unit + live round-trip); ruff clean. Opus implementers per cycle + separate opus reviewers (final APPROVE-WITH-NITS, nits applied). DISCOVERED: bug d843 (unbounded acli subprocess) + bug 4a48 (external-test leak, FIXED/closed).
